title: Largest Common Element in Two Arrays

content.problemStatement: You are given two arrays of positive integers, arr1arr1arr1 and arr2arr2arr2, of sizes nnn and mmm respectively. Your task is to find the largest common element that appears in both arrays. If no common element exists, return −1-1−1.
constraints: Sum of (n + m) over all test cases does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of: a line with two integers n and m, the sizes of the two arrays; a line with n space‑separated positive integers (arr1); a line with m space‑separated positive integers (arr2).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer – the largest element that appears in both arrays, or -1 if there is no common element.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: common elements are 3,6,7 → largest is 7.
Test case 2: arrays share no element → -1.
Test case 3: common elements are 2 and 9 → largest is 9.
Both arrays contain only the element 5, so the largest common element is 5.
There is no integer that appears in both arrays, therefore the answer is -1.

solution.approach: For each test case we store all elements of the smaller array in a hash set (O(min(n,m)) space). Then we scan the larger array and keep the maximum value that is present in the set. This yields the largest common element. Using the smaller array for the set minimises memory and improves cache performance. The overall time per test case is O(n + m) and the extra space is O(min(n,m)).
solution.timeComplexity: O(n + m) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(min(n, m)) for the hash set.
